H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble (Over-stimulation, DESCENT) achieves n=95 (largest in library), mean=7.15, std=1.78 (strong confidence). 66% pct_pos. Distribution peaks at 7-9; qualitative (n=29): 12 Noticeable, 7 Slight, 4 Moderate improvement; settle_time modes 10 min (11) and 5 min (7). AutoGluon predicts 6.859 for overstim zone (-0.29 vs observed) with X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 cross-bucket. YouTube: 30 views (highest in dataset), NULL retention. Source mix: site:26, unknown:69. Panel converged: exceptional response consistency, discovery is primary growth lever — protocol works, finding ears is the challenge.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: The overstimulation protocol consistently helps listeners reduce sensory overload, with most noticing improvement within 10 minutes.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall (Over-stimulation, DESCENT) n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73 (strong confidence). Concentrated 6-9; 65% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=21): 10 Noticeable, 3 Slight, 3 Significant; 10/21 settle ~10 min. YouTube: 5 views. Strong positive signal correlating with L1; slightly lower mean but tighter qualitative consensus on Noticeable improvement.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: This rainfall rhythm offers reliable calm for overstimulated listeners, with most reporting positive shifts.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier (Over-stimulation, DESCENT) n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68 (meaningful confidence). Left-skewed, most scores 6-9; 64% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=23): 10 Noticeable, 4 Moderate, 4 Significant; 5 min settle mode (7). YouTube: 8 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.784. Consistent with overstimulation family; panel noted L3 may benefit from clearer session structure guidance given variable settle reports.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners find the glacier rhythm effective for winding down, often feeling settled within 5 minutes.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Meadow (Anxiety & Stress, DESCENT) achieves n=67 with mean=7.22, std=1.93 (strong confidence) — the highest-observed mean in the HEALING series. Distribution shows mild right skew with mode at 7-8; 63% pct_pos. Qualitative data (n=23) confirms practical efficacy: 10 Moderate + 8 Noticeable + 2 Significant improvement, with settle_time mode at 10 min (9) and 5 min (6). AutoGluon predicts 7.838 for anxiety zone — a 0.62-point uplift over observed, suggesting untapped efficacy at scale. YouTube reach minimal (7 views, NULL retention). Source mix: adviser:5, unknown:62. Panel converged on this as the strongest anxiety protocol; no material disagreements.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners with anxiety or stress find this protocol noticeably helpful, usually settling in within 5 to 10 minutes.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage (Anxiety & Stress, DESCENT) at n=48 yields mean=6.94, std=1.62 (meaningful confidence). Distribution concentrates at 6-8 (34/48 responses); 67% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=20): 6 each Moderate/Noticeable, 5 Slight improvement — more mixed than L1. Notably 5/20 neutral music opinions vs L1's stronger reception. YouTube: 6 views, NULL retention. AutoGluon does not surface cross-bucket routing. Panel noted L2 may need stronger musical character to differentiate from L1; one analyzer flagged high cross-run drift (sim=0.652) suggesting evolving response profile as n grows.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: This gentler anxiety protocol shows promise — many feel moderate relief, though some find the music less distinctive than the first level.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley (Anxiety & Stress, DESCENT) shows n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89 (meaningful confidence). Distribution notably flat across scores 4-10 (no clear mode), indicating variable entrainment success. 61% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=20): 8 Noticeable, 7 Moderate, 2 No effect; 10/20 settle ~10 min. YouTube: 4 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.695 — notable. Panel divided on whether flat distribution reflects population heterogeneity or protocol design; hedged reading: warrants monitoring as n approaches 50.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners report calming effects that build over about 10 minutes, with results varying more person-to-person than the other anxiety protocols.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor (Acute Distress, DESCENT) at n=43 yields mean=6.35, std=1.70 (meaningful confidence) — weakest crisis protocol. Distribution bimodal-ish with peaks at 7-8 and 9; 53% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=24): 8 Significant, 7 Noticeable, 5 Moderate improvement; 8/24 settle within 5 min (fastest in crisis series). AutoGluon predicts 7.955 for crisis zone (+1.6 vs observed) and routes X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 as cross-bucket. YouTube: 6 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.733. Convergent signal: underperforms relative to crisis siblings; may need stronger early entrainment given rapid settle but lower overall scores.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: People in acute distress report meaningful relief, though results vary more widely than in other calming protocols.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor (Acute Distress, DESCENT) n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70 (meaningful confidence). Right-skewed distribution; 55% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=16): 5 Significant, 6 Moderate, 4 Noticeable; mixed settle times (5 min and 10 min clusters — bimodal recovery pattern flagged by multiple analyzers). YouTube: 4 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.662 (notable). Performing near design intent for crisis intervention; one analyzer flagged analytical drift as potential signal of population diversification.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: This harbor protocol brings noticeable calm for many listeners even in high-stress moments, with some settling quickly and others taking longer.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon (Acute Distress, DESCENT) n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91 (meaningful confidence). Symmetrical with small high tail; 52% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=19): 6 Noticeable, 5 Moderate, 4 Significant; 7/19 settle immediately — fastest crisis protocol. YouTube: 5 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.711. Consistent with crisis protocol expectation: lower means structurally expected as listeners arrive in extreme states. Panel agreed this aligns with design; no action signaled.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners under pressure find this rhythm settles them quickly — some immediately — with a range of improvement levels reported.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern (Sleep & Rest, DESCENT) n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86 (strong confidence). Multimodal distribution (peaks 7,8,9); 65% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=15): 6 Noticeable, 5 Moderate, 3 Significant; 5 min and immediate settle modes. AutoGluon predicts 7.539 for sleep zone (+0.35) with X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 cross-bucket. YouTube: 4 views. Source: unknown:54. Panel agreed: strongest sleep protocol with broad efficacy across listener types; cross-bucket signal suggests versatility.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: This lantern track helps many listeners ease into rest, with feedback showing consistent sleep support.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock (Sleep & Rest, DESCENT) n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55 (meaningful confidence) — tightest distribution in HEALING. 67% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=15): 6 Noticeable, 4 Slight, 2 No effect; 10 min settle mode (7). Notably 6/15 neutral music opinions. YouTube: 5 views. Source mix includes caregiver/patient listeners (6/4/4). Panel noted higher caregiver representation may explain neutral music ratings; efficacy signal remains solid.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: The hammock rhythm promotes restful states for most listeners, often within 10 minutes of listening.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight (Sleep & Rest, DESCENT) n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68 (meaningful confidence). Symmetrical; 64% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=22): 7 Noticeable, 5 Moderate, 5 Significant; 5 min (8) and 10 min (5) settle. YouTube: 3 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.785. Consistent performer; one analyzer suggested volume divergence check when multi-volume data arrives.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners report this midnight protocol aids relaxation and sleep preparation, with moderate to strong results.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass (Focus & Clarity, HOLD) n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82 (meaningful confidence) — notably lowest in Focus series. Broad distribution; 56% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=23): 10 Noticeable, 6 Significant, 5 Moderate; 5 min (7) and immediate (6) settle. Critical signal: 9/23 rate rhythm "Slightly busy" — most rhythm-rejected protocol in dataset. AutoGluon cross-bucket: X-ME-L1, X-MD-L1. YouTube: 5 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.804. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Rhythmic complexity undermines Focus intent at entry level; reduce layer count, defer density to L2/L3.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus outcomes vary — some listeners notice strong improvement while others feel the rhythm is a bit busy for concentration.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint (Focus & Clarity, HOLD) n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60 (meaningful confidence). Left-skewed, majority 6-8; 66% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=17): 5 each Moderate/Significant/Slight; 10 min settle (9). YouTube: 4 views. Strong middle-level performer; panel noted clear level gradient emerging in Focus series.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: This waypoint rhythm reliably supports concentration for many listeners, with moderate to significant feedback.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit (Focus & Clarity, HOLD) n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97 (meaningful confidence) — peak THRIVING score. Wider variance than L1/L2; 71% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=29): 11 Significant, 7 Noticeable, 5 Moderate; 10 min settle (15). AutoGluon predicts 7.728 for focus zone (+0.1 vs observed) with X-ME-L1/X-MD-L1 cross-bucket. YouTube: 15 views (highest in THRIVING). Cross-run drift sim=0.767. Panel: high efficacy with wider spread suggests L3 benefits subset strongly; consider hold phase extension to match long settle times.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: The summit protocol stands out for focus, with high listener ratings and strong reported improvements.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember (Creative Flow, HOLD) n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73 (meaningful confidence). Left-skewed; 53% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=21): 8 Slight, 5 Moderate, 5 Significant; immediate (6) and 10 min (6) settle modes. YouTube: 2 views. Panel converged: Creative Flow protocols underperform relative to THRIVING series average; inconsistent entrainment across arc warrants protocol tuning.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Creative flow listeners experience slight to moderate enhancement, with some noting significant improvement.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current (Creative Flow, HOLD) n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41 (meaningful confidence). Symmetrical, low variance; 58% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=15): 5 Significant, 4 Moderate, 3 Noticeable; 10 min settle (5). YouTube: 2 views. Consistent with L1 but slightly higher mean; panel noted low n limits confidence.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: This current rhythm supports creative states, with many listeners reporting meaningful positive shifts.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora (Creative Flow, HOLD) n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32 (developing confidence). Right-skewed, scores 6-9; 67% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=25): 7 Noticeable, 6 Significant, 5 Moderate; 10 min (7) and 5 min (7) settle. YouTube: 7 views. Highest mean in Creative Flow but lowest n; panel cautioned: developing signal only, may regress toward series mean as n grows.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners find this aurora track beneficial for creative focus, though feedback is still early.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone (Maintenance & Balance, HOLD) n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34 (meaningful confidence). Concentrated, low variance; 64% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=23): 6 each Moderate/Noticeable/Significant; 20 min settle mode (9) — notably long. YouTube: 3 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.739. Panel noted long settle time may reflect HOLD design intent; AutoGluon predicts 7.693 for maintenance zone (+0.65) with X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 cross-bucket — potential undertapped efficacy.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Maintenance and balance feedback is positive, with listeners often settling into a steady state over a longer session.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis (Maintenance & Balance, HOLD) n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03 (meaningful confidence) — highest variance in THRIVING. Spread distribution; 59% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=16): 5 Noticeable, 4 Moderate, 3 Slight; 5 min (6) and 10 min (4) settle. YouTube: 4 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.785. One analyzer suggested increase hold phase and re-test; panel divided on whether variance reflects population heterogeneity or irregular entrainment.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: This axis rhythm shows mixed but generally positive results for maintaining balance.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit (Maintenance & Balance, HOLD) n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73 (developing confidence). Bimodal peaks at 8 and 10; 71% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=19): 6 Noticeable, 4 Moderate, 3 Significant; 5 min settle (8), 4 "Did NOT settle". YouTube: 3 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.743. Low n, bimodal, some non-settlers — developing signal only; AutoGluon predicts 7.693 (+0.76).
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Early feedback suggests this orbit track aids balance, though some listeners don't fully settle.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze (Mood & Energy, ASCENT) n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94 (meaningful confidence). Symmetrical; 54% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=22): 7 Moderate, 6 Significant, 6 Noticeable; 20 min (6) and 10 min (5) settle. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 for low zone (+1.49 vs observed) — largest prediction gap. Universal cross-bucket: appears in 6/9 zone predictions. YouTube: 8 views. Source: adviser:6, unknown:33. Cross-run drift sim=0.818. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] AutoGluon sees universal versatility; observed scores lag — run controlled routing test for overstim/anxiety populations.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood and energy responses are moderate, with some listeners experiencing significant uplift and others mild improvement.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise (Mood & Energy, ASCENT) n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71 (meaningful confidence). Left-skewed; 64% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=15): 7 Significant, 4 Moderate, 2 Noticeable; 20 min (4) and immediate (4) settle. YouTube: 8 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.532 (very high). AutoGluon cross-bucket: 5 zones. Panel: high drift + cross-bucket breadth suggests evolving response profile; warrants monitoring.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: This sunrise rhythm brings noticeable energy for many listeners, though effects vary.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft (Mood & Energy, ASCENT) n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74 (developing confidence). Right-skewed; 56% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=25): 9 Noticeable, 6 Significant, 5 No effect (highest in TRANSFORMING); 5 min (8) and 10 min (6) settle. YouTube: 4 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.627. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Only ASCENT protocol with declining scores across levels (6.74→6.76→6.28); investigate L3 duration vs entrainment window, consider shortening or mid-protocol hold.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Early feedback suggests this updraft design can lift mood, though consistency is still developing.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath (Motivation & Drive, ASCENT) n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01 (meaningful confidence). Broad distribution; 45% pct_pos (lowest in dataset). Qualitative (n=17): 6 Noticeable, 5 Significant, 4 Moderate; 5 min (9) and immediate (4) settle. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 for motivation zone (+1.52 vs observed) with X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 cross-bucket. YouTube: 5 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.647. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Largest AutoGluon-observed gap in library; ascent arc may be too subtle at entry — increase early hold phase.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation outcomes show a wide range, with some listeners experiencing noticeable drive and others more subtle shifts.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail (Motivation & Drive, ASCENT) n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63 (developing confidence). Left-skewed; 43% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=21): 6 Noticeable, 5 Moderate, 5 Slight; 20 min (5) and 10 min (5) settle. YouTube: 7 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.585 (notable). Diverges sharply from Switchback (62% pct_pos); AutoGluon cross-bucket routing suggests misrouting. Panel: possible misrouting in current version; investigate adviser logic.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: This trail rhythm yields developing results for motivation, with moderate listener feedback.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback (Motivation & Drive, ASCENT) n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38 (developing confidence). High variance, bimodal-ish; 62% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=17): 8 Moderate, 5 Noticeable, 2 Slight; 5 min (5) and immediate (4) settle. YouTube: 7 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.507 (highest in dataset). Only Motivation L3 with strong signal but extreme drift — panel hedged: may reflect small-n instability or genuine population split; track closely.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners report variable but often positive motivation boosts from this switchback design.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern (Mind & Clarity, DESCENT) n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62 (meaningful confidence). Symmetrical; 54% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=25): 10 Noticeable, 8 Moderate, 2 "made things worse" — only protocol with negative reports. YouTube: 8 views. AutoGluon cross-bucket: X-ME-L1, X-ME-L2 (routes away from its zone). Cross-run drift sim=0.655 (notable). Panel: cross-bucket routing divergence plus negative reports warrant investigation — possible mismatch between protocol and Mind & Clarity framing.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mind clarity outcomes are moderate — some find it clarifying, a few felt it made things worse.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight (Mind & Clarity, DESCENT) n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64 (developing confidence). Left-skewed; 56% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=20): 7 Significant, 6 Moderate, 3 No effect; 10 min and immediate settle modes. YouTube: 12 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.764. Developing signal; qualitative shows stronger improvement ratings than L1 despite lower n.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: This starlight rhythm offers meaningful clarity for some listeners, though results are still developing.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos (Mind & Clarity, DESCENT) n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45 (developing confidence) — lowest mean, highest std in entire dataset. Scattered distribution; 50% pct_pos (chance level). Qualitative (n=17): 9 Noticeable, 3 Moderate, 3 Slight; 5 min (6) and 10 min (4) settle. 4/17 rate rhythm "Too busy". YouTube: 8 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.696. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Extreme variance + lowest mean = population mismatch or design failure; run population-split analysis, consider rhythm simplification or gentler L3 variant.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Feedback on this cosmos protocol is mixed — some find it clarifying, others less so, and a few find the rhythm too busy.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The 1000-outcome dataset shows The Drum Protocols operating within design expectations: HEALING and THRIVING series converge near 7.0 mean with HEALING showing superior consistency (std=1.77 vs 1.72), while TRANSFORMING trails at 6.59 with highest variance (std=1.87) — structurally consistent with its ascending-arc targeting of low-affect states.
• Global mean 6.86 across 30 protocols indicates baseline efficacy is established
• YouTube reach remains minimal (<30 views/protocol) creating a strong discovery signal: high outcomes with near-zero platform exposure
• AutoGluon identifies avg_view_percentage and avg_view_duration as the two strongest outcome predictors — above any protocol design parameter
• Listener diversity (self, caregiver, clinician) introduces unmodelled variance that limits subgroup precision
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each series exhibits a distinct efficacy fingerprint aligned with its protocol architecture and target population.
• HEALING: 12 DESCENT protocols cluster tightly (means 6.35–7.22); crisis entries structurally lower but show strong responder subgroups in qualitative data
• THRIVING: 9 HOLD protocols display a clear level gradient — L3 entries consistently outperform L1, suggesting cumulative entrainment benefits (Summit 7.63 vs Compass 6.46)
• TRANSFORMING: 9 ASCENT/DESCENT protocols show the widest internal spread and largest AutoGluon prediction–observed gaps (up to 1.5+ points in Motivation & Drive); ASCENT type appears most design-challenged with pct_pos as low as 43%
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Multiple protocols deviate from design expectations in ways that warrant targeted investigation rather than immediate revision.
• X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10): lowest mean (5.7), highest std (2.45), 50% pct_pos — only protocol at chance-level efficacy; scattered distribution suggests population mismatch or bimodality
• X-MD-L1 (Footpath, n=22) & X-MD-L2 (Trail, n=14): lowest pct_pos in dataset (45%, 43%) for ASCENT protocols — divergent from Switchback's 62% despite similar design
• X-ME-L3 (Updraft, n=18): only ASCENT protocol where outcomes decline across levels (6.74 → 6.76 → 6.28), contradicting ascending-arc design; carries highest "No effect" count (5) in TRANSFORMING
• T-FC-L1 (Compass, n=41): 9/23 qualitative respondents rate rhythm "Slightly busy" — most rhythm-rejected protocol, problematic for Focus entry point
• X-MC-L1 (Cavern, n=24): only protocol with explicit "made things worse" responses (2), a signal normally absent even from crisis protocols
• AutoGluon cross-bucket routing places X-ME-L1 and X-ME-L2 in 6 and 5 zones respectively — suggesting broader applicability than categorization implies
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Convergent signals across multiple analyzers identify specific, actionable protocol adjustments.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10) — extreme variance and lowest mean suggest population mismatch; run population-split analysis on respondent type, consider rhythm simplification (4 "Too busy" reports), or create gentler L3 variant with reduced rhythmic density
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MD-L1 (Footpath, n=22) — lowest pct_pos (45%) for Motivation ASCENT suggests ascent arc too subtle at entry; increase hold phase early in protocol to give listeners more settling time before upward movement
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L3 (Updraft, n=18) — declining scores across Mood & Energy levels contradict ASCENT design; investigate whether L3 duration exceeds optimal entrainment window, consider shortening or adding mid-protocol hold phase
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L1 (Compass, n=41) — dominant "Slightly busy" rhythm perception (9/23) undermines Focus intent; reduce rhythmic complexity or layer count for entry-level protocol, defer denser patterns to L2/L3
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-ME-L1 (Breeze, n=39) — AutoGluon's universal cross-bucket routing suggests untapped versatility; run controlled routing test offering Breeze to overstim and anxiety populations alongside native low-mood zone
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-CR-L1 (Anchor, n=43) — weakest crisis protocol (mean=6.35, pct_pos=53%) despite 8/24 listeners settling within 5 min; may need stronger entrainment in first 5 minutes given acute distress population
• THRIVING Creative Flow arc (Ember 6.74, Current 6.79, Aurora 7.28) shows inconsistent entrainment — protocol tuning warranted across the three levels
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Cross-run drift analysis reveals significant analytical instability concentrated in TRANSFORMING and crisis protocols, suggesting genuine pattern evolution rather than analytical error.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: Six most-drifted protocols (sim < 0.66) are all TRANSFORMING (X-MD-L3 0.507, X-ME-L2 0.532, X-MD-L2 0.585, X-ME-L3 0.627, X-MD-L1 0.647, X-MC-L1 0.655) plus HEALING anxiety/crisis entries (H-AX-L2 0.652, H-CR-L2 0.662, H-AX-L1 0.665, H-SL-L1 0.689, H-AX-L3 0.695) — this pattern aligns with TRANSFORMING's higher outcome variance and may reflect evolving listener populations
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient protocol embeddings for cluster analysis this run; embeddings only available for 27/30 protocols
• Confidence: Drift signals in TRANSFORMING protocols are likely meaningful given convergence with outcome variance; HEALING drift may reflect sample diversification as n grows

Qualitative (n=19): 6 Noticeable, 5 Moderate, 4 Significant; 7/19 settle immediately — fastest crisis protocol. YouTube: 5 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.711. Consistent with crisis protocol expectation: lower means structurally expected as listeners arrive in extreme states. Panel agreed this aligns with design; no action signaled.
by_protocol.H-OS-L1: Pebble (Over-stimulation, DESCENT) achieves n=95 (largest in library), mean=7.15, std=1.78 (strong confidence). 66% pct_pos. Distribution peaks at 7-9; qualitative (n=29): 12 Noticeable, 7 Slight, 4 Moderate improvement; settle_time modes 10 min (11) and 5 min (7). AutoGluon predicts 6.859 for overstim zone (-0.29 vs observed) with X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 cross-bucket. YouTube: 30 views (highest in dataset), NULL retention. Source mix: site:26, unknown:69. Panel converged: exceptional response consistency, discovery is primary growth lever — protocol works, finding ears is the challenge.
by_protocol.H-OS-L2: Rainfall (Over-stimulation, DESCENT) n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73 (strong confidence). Concentrated 6-9; 65% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=21): 10 Noticeable, 3 Slight, 3 Significant; 10/21 settle ~10 min. YouTube: 5 views. Strong positive signal correlating with L1; slightly lower mean but tighter qualitative consensus on Noticeable improvement.
by_protocol.H-OS-L3: Glacier (Over-stimulation, DESCENT) n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68 (meaningful confidence). Left-skewed, most scores 6-9; 64% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=23): 10 Noticeable, 4 Moderate, 4 Significant; 5 min settle mode (7). YouTube: 8 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.784. Consistent with overstimulation family; panel noted L3 may benefit from clearer session structure guidance given variable settle reports.
by_protocol.H-SL-L1: Lantern (Sleep & Rest, DESCENT) n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86 (strong confidence). Multimodal distribution (peaks 7,8,9); 65% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=15): 6 Noticeable, 5 Moderate, 3 Significant; 5 min and immediate settle modes. AutoGluon predicts 7.539 for sleep zone (+0.35) with X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 cross-bucket. YouTube: 4 views. Source: unknown:54. Panel agreed: strongest sleep protocol with broad efficacy across listener types; cross-bucket signal suggests versatility.
by_protocol.H-SL-L2: Hammock (Sleep & Rest, DESCENT) n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55 (meaningful confidence) — tightest distribution in HEALING. 67% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=15): 6 Noticeable, 4 Slight, 2 No effect; 10 min settle mode (7). Notably 6/15 neutral music opinions. YouTube: 5 views. Source mix includes caregiver/patient listeners (6/4/4). Panel noted higher caregiver representation may explain neutral music ratings; efficacy signal remains solid.
by_protocol.H-SL-L3: Midnight (Sleep & Rest, DESCENT) n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68 (meaningful confidence). Symmetrical; 64% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=22): 7 Noticeable, 5 Moderate, 5 Significant; 5 min (8) and 10 min (5) settle. YouTube: 3 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.785. Consistent performer; one analyzer suggested volume divergence check when multi-volume data arrives.
by_protocol.T-CF-L1: Ember (Creative Flow, HOLD) n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73 (meaningful confidence). Left-skewed; 53% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=21): 8 Slight, 5 Moderate, 5 Significant; immediate (6) and 10 min (6) settle modes. YouTube: 2 views. Panel converged: Creative Flow protocols underperform relative to THRIVING series average; inconsistent entrainment across arc warrants protocol tuning.
by_protocol.T-CF-L2: Current (Creative Flow, HOLD) n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41 (meaningful confidence). Symmetrical, low variance; 58% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=15): 5 Significant, 4 Moderate, 3 Noticeable; 10 min settle (5). YouTube: 2 views. Consistent with L1 but slightly higher mean; panel noted low n limits confidence.
by_protocol.T-CF-L3: Aurora (Creative Flow, HOLD) n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32 (developing confidence). Right-skewed, scores 6-9; 67% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=25): 7 Noticeable, 6 Significant, 5 Moderate; 10 min (7) and 5 min (7) settle. YouTube: 7 views. Highest mean in Creative Flow but lowest n; panel cautioned: developing signal only, may regress toward series mean as n grows.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1: Compass (Focus & Clarity, HOLD) n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82 (meaningful confidence) — notably lowest in Focus series. Broad distribution; 56% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=23): 10 Noticeable, 6 Significant, 5 Moderate; 5 min (7) and immediate (6) settle. Critical signal: 9/23 rate rhythm "Slightly busy" — most rhythm-rejected protocol in dataset. AutoGluon cross-bucket: X-ME-L1, X-MD-L1. YouTube: 5 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.804. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Rhythmic complexity undermines Focus intent at entry level; reduce layer count, defer density to L2/L3.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2: Waypoint (Focus & Clarity, HOLD) n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60 (meaningful confidence). Left-skewed, majority 6-8; 66% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=17): 5 each Moderate/Significant/Slight; 10 min settle (9). YouTube: 4 views. Strong middle-level performer; panel noted clear level gradient emerging in Focus series.
by_protocol.T-FC-L3: Summit (Focus & Clarity, HOLD) n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97 (meaningful confidence) — peak THRIVING score. Wider variance than L1/L2; 71% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=29): 11 Significant, 7 Noticeable, 5 Moderate; 10 min settle (15). AutoGluon predicts 7.728 for focus zone (+0.1 vs observed) with X-ME-L1/X-MD-L1 cross-bucket. YouTube: 15 views (highest in THRIVING). Cross-run drift sim=0.767. Panel: high efficacy with wider spread suggests L3 benefits subset strongly; consider hold phase extension to match long settle times.
by_protocol.T-MB-L1: Stone (Maintenance & Balance, HOLD) n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34 (meaningful confidence). Concentrated, low variance; 64% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=23): 6 each Moderate/Noticeable/Significant; 20 min settle mode (9) — notably long. YouTube: 3 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.739. Panel noted long settle time may reflect HOLD design intent; AutoGluon predicts 7.693 for maintenance zone (+0.65) with X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 cross-bucket — potential undertapped efficacy.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: Axis (Maintenance & Balance, HOLD) n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03 (meaningful confidence) — highest variance in THRIVING. Spread distribution; 59% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=16): 5 Noticeable, 4 Moderate, 3 Slight; 5 min (6) and 10 min (4) settle. YouTube: 4 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.785. One analyzer suggested increase hold phase and re-test; panel divided on whether variance reflects population heterogeneity or irregular entrainment.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: Orbit (Maintenance & Balance, HOLD) n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73 (developing confidence). Bimodal peaks at 8 and 10; 71% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=19): 6 Noticeable, 4 Moderate, 3 Significant; 5 min settle (8), 4 "Did NOT settle". YouTube: 3 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.743. Low n, bimodal, some non-settlers — developing signal only; AutoGluon predicts 7.693 (+0.76).
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: Cavern (Mind & Clarity, DESCENT) n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62 (meaningful confidence). Symmetrical; 54% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=25): 10 Noticeable, 8 Moderate, 2 "made things worse" — only protocol with negative reports. YouTube: 8 views. AutoGluon cross-bucket: X-ME-L1, X-ME-L2 (routes away from its zone). Cross-run drift sim=0.655 (notable). Panel: cross-bucket routing divergence plus negative reports warrant investigation — possible mismatch between protocol and Mind & Clarity framing.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: Starlight (Mind & Clarity, DESCENT) n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64 (developing confidence). Left-skewed; 56% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=20): 7 Significant, 6 Moderate, 3 No effect; 10 min and immediate settle modes. YouTube: 12 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.764. Developing signal; qualitative shows stronger improvement ratings than L1 despite lower n.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: Cosmos (Mind & Clarity, DESCENT) n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45 (developing confidence) — lowest mean, highest std in entire dataset. Scattered distribution; 50% pct_pos (chance level). Qualitative (n=17): 9 Noticeable, 3 Moderate, 3 Slight; 5 min (6) and 10 min (4) settle. 4/17 rate rhythm "Too busy". YouTube: 8 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.696. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Extreme variance + lowest mean = population mismatch or design failure; run population-split analysis, consider rhythm simplification or gentler L3 variant.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: Footpath (Motivation & Drive, ASCENT) n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01 (meaningful confidence). Broad distribution; 45% pct_pos (lowest in dataset). Qualitative (n=17): 6 Noticeable, 5 Significant, 4 Moderate; 5 min (9) and immediate (4) settle. AutoGluon predicts 7.876 for motivation zone (+1.52 vs observed) with X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 cross-bucket. YouTube: 5 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.647. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Largest AutoGluon-observed gap in library; ascent arc may be too subtle at entry — increase early hold phase.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: Trail (Motivation & Drive, ASCENT) n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63 (developing confidence). Left-skewed; 43% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=21): 6 Noticeable, 5 Moderate, 5 Slight; 20 min (5) and 10 min (5) settle. YouTube: 7 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.585 (notable). Diverges sharply from Switchback (62% pct_pos); AutoGluon cross-bucket routing suggests misrouting. Panel: possible misrouting in current version; investigate adviser logic.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: Switchback (Motivation & Drive, ASCENT) n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38 (developing confidence). High variance, bimodal-ish; 62% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=17): 8 Moderate, 5 Noticeable, 2 Slight; 5 min (5) and immediate (4) settle. YouTube: 7 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.507 (highest in dataset). Only Motivation L3 with strong signal but extreme drift — panel hedged: may reflect small-n instability or genuine population split; track closely.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: Breeze (Mood & Energy, ASCENT) n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94 (meaningful confidence). Symmetrical; 54% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=22): 7 Moderate, 6 Significant, 6 Noticeable; 20 min (6) and 10 min (5) settle. AutoGluon predicts 8.230 for low zone (+1.49 vs observed) — largest prediction gap. Universal cross-bucket: appears in 6/9 zone predictions. YouTube: 8 views. Source: adviser:6, unknown:33. Cross-run drift sim=0.818. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] AutoGluon sees universal versatility; observed scores lag — run controlled routing test for overstim/anxiety populations.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: Sunrise (Mood & Energy, ASCENT) n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71 (meaningful confidence). Left-skewed; 64% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=15): 7 Significant, 4 Moderate, 2 Noticeable; 20 min (4) and immediate (4) settle. YouTube: 8 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.532 (very high). AutoGluon cross-bucket: 5 zones. Panel: high drift + cross-bucket breadth suggests evolving response profile; warrants monitoring.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: Updraft (Mood & Energy, ASCENT) n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74 (developing confidence). Right-skewed; 56% pct_pos. Qualitative (n=25): 9 Noticeable, 6 Significant, 5 No effect (highest in TRANSFORMING); 5 min (8) and 10 min (6) settle. YouTube: 4 views. Cross-run drift sim=0.627. [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Only ASCENT protocol with declining scores across levels (6.74→6.76→6.28); investigate L3 duration vs entrainment window, consider shortening or mid-protocol hold.
by_protocol.H-AX-L1-PLAIN: Most listeners with anxiety or stress find this protocol noticeably helpful, usually settling in within 5 to 10 minutes.
by_protocol.H-AX-L2-PLAIN: This gentler anxiety protocol shows promise — many feel moderate relief, though some find the music less distinctive than the first level.
by_protocol.H-AX-L3-PLAIN: Listeners report calming effects that build over about 10 minutes, with results varying more person-to-person than the other anxiety protocols.
by_protocol.H-CR-L1-PLAIN: People in acute distress report meaningful relief, though results vary more widely than in other calming protocols.
by_protocol.H-CR-L2-PLAIN: This harbor protocol brings noticeable calm for many listeners even in high-stress moments, with some settling quickly and others taking longer.
by_protocol.H-CR-L3-PLAIN: Listeners under pressure find this rhythm settles them quickly — some immediately — with a range of improvement levels reported.
by_protocol.H-OS-L1-PLAIN: The overstimulation protocol consistently helps listeners reduce sensory overload, with most noticing improvement within 10 minutes.
by_protocol.H-OS-L2-PLAIN: This rainfall rhythm offers reliable calm for overstimulated listeners, with most reporting positive shifts.
by_protocol.H-OS-L3-PLAIN: Listeners find the glacier rhythm effective for winding down, often feeling settled within 5 minutes.
by_protocol.H-SL-L1-PLAIN: This lantern track helps many listeners ease into rest, with feedback showing consistent sleep support.
by_protocol.H-SL-L2-PLAIN: The hammock rhythm promotes restful states for most listeners, often within 10 minutes of listening.
by_protocol.H-SL-L3-PLAIN: Listeners report this midnight protocol aids relaxation and sleep preparation, with moderate to strong results.
by_protocol.T-CF-L1-PLAIN: Creative flow listeners experience slight to moderate enhancement, with some noting significant improvement.
by_protocol.T-CF-L2-PLAIN: This current rhythm supports creative states, with many listeners reporting meaningful positive shifts.
by_protocol.T-CF-L3-PLAIN: Listeners find this aurora track beneficial for creative focus, though feedback is still early.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1-PLAIN: Focus outcomes vary — some listeners notice strong improvement while others feel the rhythm is a bit busy for concentration.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2-PLAIN: This waypoint rhythm reliably supports concentration for many listeners, with moderate to significant feedback.
by_protocol.T-FC-L3-PLAIN: The summit protocol stands out for focus, with high listener ratings and strong reported improvements.
by_protocol.T-MB-L1-PLAIN: Maintenance and balance feedback is positive, with listeners often settling into a steady state over a longer session.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2-PLAIN: This axis rhythm shows mixed but generally positive results for maintaining balance.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3-PLAIN: Early feedback suggests this orbit track aids balance, though some listeners don't fully settle.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1-PLAIN: Mind clarity outcomes are moderate — some find it clarifying, a few felt it made things worse.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2-PLAIN: This starlight rhythm offers meaningful clarity for some listeners, though results are still developing.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3-PLAIN: Feedback on this cosmos protocol is mixed — some find it clarifying, others less so, and a few find the rhythm too busy.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1-PLAIN: Motivation outcomes show a wide range, with some listeners experiencing noticeable drive and others more subtle shifts.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2-PLAIN: This trail rhythm yields developing results for motivation, with moderate listener feedback.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3-PLAIN: Listeners report variable but often positive motivation boosts from this switchback design.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1-PLAIN: Mood and energy responses are moderate, with some listeners experiencing significant uplift and others mild improvement.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2-PLAIN: This sunrise rhythm brings noticeable energy for many listeners, though effects vary.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3-PLAIN: Early feedback suggests this updraft design can lift mood, though consistency is still developing.
